title: Supreme Court quashes criminal proceedings against Ashoka University professor after Haryana refuses prosecution sanction

title: Plugging in: Why India must electrify its kitchens at scale

title: Light to moderate rain likely in Hyderabad on March 16, says IMD

title: Watch: The ruling party and the opposition must rise above partisanship | The Hindu Editorial

title: No Eid celebrations for Shias in MP in the wake of Khamenei's death in the Iran-Iraq War

The options were ‘Pandit’ (scholar), ‘Avsarvadi’ (opportunist), ‘Nishkapat’ (innocent or sincere) and ‘Sadachari’ (virtuous). CM issues directions to avoid ‘derogatory’ and ‘disrespectful’ references

title: Diversify agriculture and encourage farmers to grow pulses, Supreme Court tells government

title: Over 900 chicken pox cases reported in Ernakulam in 2026

title: Young birdies 17, wins Players on Fitzpatrick miss
content: Cameron Young made a tying 9½-foot putt for birdie on 17 and then won the Players Championship on 18 after Matt Fitzpatrick missed an 8-foot par putt.

title: Sources: Goedert stays with Eagles on new deal
content: Tight end Dallas Goedert reached agreement Sunday on a one-year deal to return to the Eagles for his ninth season in Philadelphia, sources told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: NBA: Missed call late in Lakers-Nuggets thriller
content: The NBA ruled that Spencer Jones had a clean strip of Austin Reaves with 9.2 seconds remaining in regulation of Saturday's Nuggets-Lakers game and that Reaves should not have gone to the free throw line.

title: The balancing act: How India is stepping up engagement with Iran as Hormuz crisis deepens
content: India is prioritizing diplomatic engagement with Iran to ensure safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, offering a stark contrast to the US military approach. New Delhi's direct talks have already yielded results, with Indian tankers resuming transit. This strategy aims to secure India's crucial energy imports while carefully managing its relationship with the United States.

title: Major reshuffle in Bengal: EC removes DGP, Kolkata CP and other top bureaucrats ahead of polls
content: The Election Commission ordered the transfer of top bureaucrats in West Bengal, including the Chief Secretary and Principal Secretary of Home and Hill Affairs, citing preparedness for upcoming assembly elections. This move led to a walkout by TMC MPs from the Rajya Sabha, who accused the EC of misusing its powers against the elected government.

title: Haryana: Husband arrested after woman and 3 daughters found dead in Nuh well
content: A husband has been arrested for abetting the suicide of his wife and three daughters found dead in a well. Allegations of severe domestic abuse, dowry harassment, and pressure for a male heir surfaced following the tragic incident. The wife's father claims she was pushed, while police investigate the husband's role and potential accomplices.

title: ‘I want to break that’: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi targets Chris Gayle’s iconic IPL record
content: Teen sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi aims to shatter Chris Gayle's iconic 175-run T20 record in the upcoming IPL season. After a stellar 2025, including a U-19 World Cup final century, the Rajasthan Royals star is focused on team victory while eyeing individual milestones. He credits the franchise and Rahul Dravid for his rapid development.

title: FASTag annual pass gets pricier: Here's what changes from April 1
content: The National Highways Authority of India is increasing the FASTag Annual Pass fee. Starting April 1, 2026, the pass will cost Rs 3,075. This prepaid toll plan is for non-commercial private vehicles. The revision aligns with National Highways Fee Rules. Over 56 lakh users have adopted the pass. The new rate applies to eligible vehicles at approximately 1,150 fee plazas.

title: US-Iran war hits stock market: Citi, Nomura cut Nifty50 target prices; here’s the outlook
content: Citi has revised its Nifty target to 27,000 from the earlier estimate of 28,500. The new projection suggests a potential upside of about 17 percent from the index’s last closing level.

title: ‘Pakistan becoming an associate team’: Ex-cricketer slams Afridi, team management
content: Ahmed Shehzad, a former Pakistan cricketer, has strongly criticized the team's leadership following their ODI series defeat to Bangladesh. He expressed concerns about the declining standards in Pakistan cricket. Shehzad questioned the management's decisions and their handling of player availability. The team's recent performance has drawn significant criticism.

title: FBI is investigating malware hidden in games on Steam: Suspected titles, safety tips for gamers and more
content: The FBI is investigating malware embedded in games on Steam, with a focus on potential victims between May 2024 and January 2026. Several games, including BlockBlasters and Chemia, have been identified as containing the malicious software. The agency is urging anyone who may have installed these games or has relevant information to come forward.

title: High speed alone can't establish rash driving: Why K'taka HC acquitted man in road accident case
content: The High Court concluded that both the trial court and the appellate court had erred in convicting the accused without sufficient evidence establishing rash and negligent driving.

title: Hardik Pandya drives Ferrari 12Cilindri In Mumbai: Why's this car special
content: Star cricketer Hardik Pandya was recently spotted driving Ferrari's newest front-engined V12 grand tourer, the 12Cilindri, in Mumbai. This stunning supercar, inspired by the legendary 365 GTB/4 Daytona, boasts a powerful 6.5-litre V12 engine producing 830 hp and can accelerate from 0 to 100 kmph in just 2.9 seconds. The 12Cilindri, priced around Rs 8.

title: Perplexity AI CEO agrees that Computer Science is gradually returning to its roots
content: AI is rapidly automating coding's routine tasks, pushing software engineering back towards its mathematical roots. Experts predict AI could soon handle most end-to-end coding, with some engineers already code-free. While LLMs still struggle with complex design, this shift is prompting a rethink in computer science education, emphasizing logic over syntax.

title: Rasgulla stuck in throat: Man chokes to death at wedding feast
content: A tragic incident unfolded at a wedding feast in Jamshedpur, Jharkhand, where a 41-year-old man, Lalit Singh, succumbed to death. A rasgulla reportedly became lodged in his throat, completely blocking his airway and cutting off oxygen supply. He collapsed during the meal and tragically passed away within minutes despite immediate efforts to seek medical assistance.

title: Teen driving at 118 kmph crashes into farmhouse wall in Gujarat, 2 dead
content: A 20-year-old man, recently released from jail, died after his speeding car crashed into a farmhouse wall in Rajkot. He had reportedly posted an Instagram story showing the car's speed at 118 kmph shortly before the fatal accident. Another occupant, a 16-year-old, also succumbed to injuries, while a third person remains hospitalized.

title: Oscars 2026 complete winners list: Paul Thomas Anderson, Michael B Jordan win big
content: At the star-studded 98th Academy Awards, a plethora of cinematic triumphs were on display, with 'One Battle After Another' stealing the spotlight by winning Best Picture alongside numerous other awards. Jessie Buckley dazzled the audience and judges alike, scoring Best Actress for her moving role in 'Hamnet', while Michael B.

title: Are Trump and Netanyahu on same page? What US President said amidst rumors of rift
content: President Trump denied reports of a rift with Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu, calling them 'fake news' and describing their relationship as 'extraordinary.' This comes amid ongoing US-Israeli military operations against Iran, which have destabilized the region and impacted global markets. While sharing short-term military goals, their long-term objectives for Iran appear to differ.

title: Watch: Nor’wester, tornado hit Odisha’s Mayurbhanj; several homes damaged, 2 dead, 29 injured
content: A severe nor'wester and tornado struck four villages in Mayurbhanj district, claiming two lives and injuring 29 others. The storm caused extensive damage to houses, uprooted trees, and blew away vehicles and people. The Chief Minister announced ex-gratia for the deceased and free treatment for the injured.

title: Gold price today: How much 18K, 22K and 24K gold cost in your city; check rates
content: Gold futures experienced a significant drop of Rs 2,225 to Rs 1.56 lakh per 10 grams, influenced by a stronger US dollar and weaker international markets. Analysts attribute the decline to rising energy prices and diminished expectations for Federal Reserve interest rate cuts, impacting investor sentiment and leading to outflows from gold-backed ETFs.

title: ‘Will you steal ICC trophies now?’ Ex-cricketer blasts Pakistan after loss to Bangladesh
content: Pakistan's cricket woes deepened with a 2-1 ODI series loss to Bangladesh, drawing sharp criticism from former wicketkeeper Kamran Akmal. Akmal questioned the team's performance and administration, highlighting their struggles after an early T20 World Cup exit. Bangladesh secured the series win in Dhaka after a strong batting performance and effective bowling.

title: Broom, wiper, belt used to torture 4-year-old? UP man, 2nd wife held after boy dies
content: Chowk police in Lucknow recovered household objects, including a broom, knife, and belt, suspected of being used to torture a four-year-old boy. The child died with 18 injury marks and multiple fractures, indicating sustained abuse. His father and stepmother have been arrested and are being questioned.

title: ‘Reason & coordinate’: Jaishankar reveals how India secured two tankers’ passage through Hormuz
content: External affairs minister S Jaishankar hailed direct talks with Iran as the most effective way to resume shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Tehran has allowed Indian-flagged vessels to pass, demonstrating the success of diplomacy over potential military action. This approach offers a potential model for other nations grappling with disrupted energy supplies.

title: US forced labour probe: China urges Trump to 'correct erroneous' trade practices
content: China has lodged formal objections with the US over new trade investigations into forced labor and excess industrial capacity, urging Washington to correct its "erroneous ways." The probes, targeting 60 economies including China, come as trade negotiators meet in Paris. Beijing criticizes the investigations as "political manipulation" and "discriminatory," while the US cites concerns over forced labor in Xinjiang.

title: Iran’s Sejjil-2 'dancing missile': The solid-fuel weapon that can strike 2,000 km away
content: Iran's IRGC launched a significant missile barrage, including the advanced Sejjil, targeting Israeli military sites in retaliation for recent strikes. This marks the first use of Sejjil missiles since the conflict escalated on February 28. The operation, dubbed 'True Promise 4', involved multiple ballistic missile types, aiming at strategic locations and defense industries.

title: Eid Ul Fitr Moon sighting 2026: When will India, Pak, Saudi Arabia, UAE and Iran celebrate?
content: The final days of Ramadan bring a familiar anticipation for the moon sighting, determining the start of Eid al-Fitr. In 2026, this could be March 20 or 21, with celebrations often split globally. Beyond the festive meals and new outfits, Eid emphasizes community, charity, and gratitude for completing the month of fasting.

title: Why are gold prices not rising too much amid US-Iran war? Key levels to watch for
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices are supported by the underlying geopolitical negative sentiment but the gains are capped, says Manav Modi, Senior Analyst, Commodity Research at Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd.

title: ‘I’ll hit it’: Samson reveals Sooryavanshi’s fearless chat with Dravid before IPL debut
content: Rajasthan Royals' young sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi surprised coach Rahul Dravid with his fearless approach before his IPL debut.  Samson revealed Sooryavanshi's confident response to Dravid's queries, stating he'd 'just play' and 'hit the first ball in the air' if in his zone.  Sooryavanshi, the youngest IPL debutant, aims to help RR win the trophy in IPL 2026.

title: Iran war impact: Oil industry leaders warn Trump admin fuel crunch may worsen
content: Top oil executives warned the Trump administration that the energy crisis stemming from the Iran conflict could worsen.  Concerns were raised about disruptions to shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil transit route.  Despite President Trump's prediction of falling oil prices once the conflict subsides, his call for allies to deploy warships has met with negative responses.

title: ‘Our forever begins now’: Kuldeep shares first post after wedding with Vanshika
content: Indian spinner Kuldeep Yadav tied the knot with childhood friend Vanshika Chaddha in a star-studded Mussoorie wedding. The private ceremony saw cricketers like Suresh Raina and Yuzvendra Chahal in attendance.  The couple, who grew up near each other in Kanpur, celebrated with traditional functions before the main event.  This marks a new chapter for the cricketer.

title: 'It's time to go now': Harish Rana's farewell as AIIMS begins passive euthanasia process
content: A 32-year-old man, Harish Rana, has been moved to AIIMS for passive euthanasia after the Supreme Court upheld his right to die with dignity. Rana has been in a vegetative state since 2013 due to severe head injuries. The process involves withdrawing life-sustaining treatment while ensuring comfort and pain relief.

title: ‘Have absolute right to charge tariffs in another form’: Trump slams Supreme Court ruling
content: President Trump criticized the Supreme Court's decision to invalidate his global tariffs, warning of trillions in losses and vowing to impose them in new forms. He thanked three justices for their stance while also criticizing Republican appointees for perceived disrespect and politicized rulings. The administration is exploring alternative legal avenues to recover lost tariff revenue.

title: Why Kharg Island matters: The oil lifeline Trump just put in his crosshairs
content: A small rocky island, Kharg Island, has become a focal point in US-Iran tensions due to its critical role in Iran's oil exports. US forces struck military installations on the island, deliberately sparing oil facilities to signal leverage without crippling Iran's economy.

title: Last-over drama! Pakistan file complaint after controversial DRS decision in Bangladesh ODI
content: Pakistan has lodged a complaint with the match referee over a controversial DRS call in the final over of their ODI loss to Bangladesh. The Pakistan camp alleges Bangladesh reviewed a decision after seeing a replay on the big screen and potentially outside the allowed time. This overturned wide call significantly impacted the match's outcome.

title: 'We catch them, but they go to CM, MLAs': Cop claims 'political pressure' to release goons
content: A Delhi Police inspector's video alleging political interference in releasing criminals has sparked controversy. The officer claimed offenders were freed due to intervention from public representatives. Delhi Police has denied these claims, calling the remarks irresponsible. The inspector has been removed from his post and transferred as disciplinary action. Police stated they operate strictly by law without external influence.

title: 25 years of India vs Australia Eden Gardens Test: And thus died the follow-on, or did it?
content: India’s fightback at the Eden Gardens did make captains and teams wary of enforcing the follow-on. Australia, even under successful captains like Ricky Ponting and Adam Gilchrist enforced it just four times out of the 14 in which they had a choice to do so. Steve Waugh though enforced it seven times post Kolkata and won every Test!

title: UAE orders action against 19 Indians over misleading posts amid Iran war
content: The UAE has ordered the arrest of 35 individuals, including 19 Indians, for allegedly spreading fabricated videos and misinformation online amid escalating regional tensions. Authorities stated these actions aimed to incite panic and undermine stability by circulating real and AI-generated clips of missile interceptions and glorifying hostile states.  The accused face expedited trials.

title: 10 dead, several injured in fire at Odisha’s SCB Medical College
content: At least 10 patients died in a fire at SCB Medical College and Hospital's trauma care ICU in Cuttack early Sunday morning. The blaze, suspected to be caused by a short circuit, led to seven deaths within the ICU and three during evacuation. Odisha's Chief Minister ordered a judicial probe into the incident.

title: Major fire breaks out at Dubai airport after drone strike; flights suspended — watch
content: Dubai International Airport faced a major fire Monday following Iranian drone attacks across Gulf states. Flight operations were temporarily suspended as civil defense teams successfully contained the blaze, preventing impact on a fuel tank. Videos showed significant flames and smoke. Regional powers condemned Iran's escalating strikes, which are increasingly threatening key Gulf cities.

title: Beyond the bling: Hardik Pandya remains India’s most reliable big-match allrounder
content: Hardik Pandya's crucial performances in India's back-to-back T20 World Cup victories in 2024 and 2026 are often overshadowed by his flamboyant lifestyle. Despite off-field distractions, Pandya delivered match-winning spells and vital batting contributions, proving an indispensable asset alongside Jasprit Bumrah. His dedication and improved game intelligence remain key to his success.

title: Stock market today: Nifty50 opens below 23,150; BSE Sensex down around 100 points
content: Stock market today: After a week of selloff, Nifty50 and BSE Sensex opened flat in trade on Monday. While Nifty50 was below 23,150, BSE Sensex was down around 100 points.

title: Oil prices continue to rise as US-Iran war rages on; Brent crude surges past $105
content: Global oil prices eased slightly as President Trump urged international cooperation to secure the Strait of Hormuz, following a sharp rally driven by US-Iran tensions.  The disruption has impacted a fifth of global oil supply, prompting the IEA to release over 400 million barrels from strategic reserves.

title: ‘Treat Pakistan matches like a comedy series’: Ex-cricketer slams team after series loss to Bangladesh
content: Former Pakistan wicketkeeper Kamran Akmal lambasted the national team after their unexpected 1-2 ODI series loss to Bangladesh.  He urged treating their matches as comedy, highlighting the disgrace of losing to a lower-ranked opponent. Bangladesh secured a historic series win, their first against Pakistan in 11 years, with standout performances from Tanzid Hasan Tamim and Taskin Ahmed.

title: US Jets Strike Military Facilities Near Iran's Chabahar Trade Zone: Report
content: US fighter jets have reportedly struck military facilities on a mountain near the Chabahar Free Trade Zone. According to a report by Voice of America, sounds of intense explosions were heard in the area behind the zone.

title: Trump Tells Allies To Escort Ships Through Hormuz. Not Everyone Is On Board
content: The United States is reportedly planning to announce a "coalition" of multiple nations to help escort ships through the war-hit Strait of Hormuz as early as this week amid soaring oil prices due to the US and Israel's war on Iran.

title: "Want Son To Come Home": Indian Student Killed By Collegemates In Canada
content: Gurkirat Singh Manocha died after being assaulted by a group of youths and allegedly run over by a vehicle in Fort St John.

title: "Like Shooting An Army In The Head": How Internet Isolation Can Hit Russia's War
content: Telegram has become a key communication tool for Russian soldiers, military bloggers and volunteer networks supporting the war effort.

title: Canada Sees Sharp 19% Drop In Immigration In 2025, Indian Arrivals Down 22%
content: Immigration to Canada dropped sharply in 2025, revealing a rare non-pandemic drop since 2015. Compared to 2024, immigration to Canada dropped 19 per cent

title: Rajya Sabha Elections LIVE Updates: Voting Underway For 37 Seats In 10 States
content: The voting started at 9:00 am and will continue till 4:00 pm. Counting of votes will start at 5:00 pm.

title: Election Commission's Reshuffle Hours After Poll Announcement, Trinamool Hits Back
content: Just hours after the West Bengal poll dates were announced, the Election Commission ordered the removal of top bureaucrats in the state, including Chief Secretary Nandini Chakravorty, of the Mamata Banerjee-led government.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates: Markets Open Flat Amid Mixed Global Cues
content: Sensex Today | Nifty 50 | Stock Market Live Updates: While the Sensex opened 50 points higher, Nifty also started the day in green.

title: Why Rajya Sabha Election In 3 States Has Become Opposition's Loyalty Test
content: Some were shepherded to hilly resorts, others buried differences and embraced at Iftar parties, and most made calls to strike unlikely alliances.

title: US-Israel-Iran War LIVE: 1 Dead After Iranian Missile Lands On Car In Abu Dhabi
content: US-Israel-Iran War News LIVE Updates: The war in the Middle East broke out when the US and Israel attacked Iran on February 28.

title: OpenAI Co-Founder Ranks Jobs By AI Risk, Musk Predicts 'Universal High Income'
content: As the world discusses and debates the impact of artificial intelligence on the job market and the resulting mass layoffs, Slovakian-Canadian researcher Andrej Karpathy may have some answers to how the technology will affect different occupations.

title: Iranian Embassy Thanks Indians For Support, Seeks Cash Donations Amid War
content: The Iranian Embassy in New Delhi has expressed gratitude to Indian citizens who have shown interest in providing humanitarian aid to Iranians, but noted that technical difficulties have made it challenging to accept contributions via online banking.

title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei Had Doubts About Son Mojtaba Leading Iran: Report
content: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei reportedly viewed son Mojtaba as inexperienced and unqualified for leadership.

title: Air India Flight Makes Precautionary Landing In Ireland Due To 'Technical Snag'
content: The flight, AI102, took off from JFK (New York) Airport and made a precautionary landing in Ireland at 4:30 pm (local time).

title: Israeli PM Netanyahu's Coffee Video Under Scrutiny, Grok Calls It 'Deepfake'
content: Amid rumours about his whereabouts, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's new video is under scrutiny, with several social media users claiming it is generated using artificial intelligence (AI).

title: Why Sensex, Nifty Opened In Green Today Despite Rise In Oil Prices
content: Indian benchmark indices opened slightly higher on Monday despite the Brent crude oil price shooting above $100 per barrel.

title: Massive Fire Near Dubai Airport After Iranian Drone Strike, Flights Suspended
content: Authorities in Dubai have contained a fire that broke out near Dubai International Airport on Monday after a drone-related incident struck a fuel tank in the area.

title: Cheap Generic Ozempic To Hit India: Patent Exit Sparks Weight-Loss Drug Boom
content: Swati Pradhan sees about 12 obesity patients a day at her clinic in southern Mumbai. She expects this number to at least double from this month as Indian drug makers prepare to launch off-patent versions of Novo Nordisk A/S's blockbuster weight.

title: Nuked In 1945, Japan To Now Build Missiles For US
content: Next week, Japan is expected to formally notify Washington of its intention to join the Golden Domeprogramme.

title: Madhya Pradesh Teacher Suspended For Mimicking PM Modi Over LPG Prices
content: A government school teacher in Madhya Pradesh's Shivpuri district was suspended after a video of him mimicking Prime Minister Narendra Modi while making satirical remarks on LPG cylinder prices went viral on social media
